errors.criterion_text: Patients with heart failure

Meets the 2021 KDIGO definition of steroid-resistant nephrotic syndrome (SRNS), and either: has received an adequate dose of calcineurin inhibitors (CNIs) for more than 6 months without achieving at least partial remission; or has contraindications to CNI use (e.g., eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73 m², prominent tubular injury on biopsy, elevated urinary markers > 3x ULN, abnormal glucose tolerance, severe uncontrolled hypertension, significant drug interactions, or known CNI allergy); or has shown inadequate response or relapse after treatment with at least two immunosuppressive agents (including a CNI and at least one other conventional or biologic agent), defined as failure to achieve complete remission after 12 months of therapy or relapse following initial response

analysis: Overall accuracy is 80% (16/20 labeled criteria matched exactly, n=40 total labels, 20 unmatched due to criterion-text drift). The weakest verdict class is UNCERTAIN at 0% recall, meaning the matcher most often mislabels this ground-truth category as something else. 1 case(s) where the true label is UNKNOWN (vignette genuinely silent) were instead called MET or NOT_MET -- the matcher's most consequential failure mode, since it manufactures certainty the source data doesn't support. The remaining 3 error(s) are boundary calls between UNCERTAIN and a definite verdict, which is a lower-stakes miss than the UNKNOWN-to-definite case. Labels were hand-written from criterion text + vignette alone, blind to the matcher's own output, so this number reflects genuine matcher accuracy rather than label leakage.
